title: Hip Pain After Fall - Femoral Neck Fracture
chief_complaint: 78-year-old female slipped on rug at home. Now severe right hip pain, unable to bear weight. Right leg shortened and externally rotated. History of osteoporosis.

physical_exam.hip: Right leg shortened and externally rotated, tenderness over hip, pain with any movement, unable to perform straight leg raise

hidden_data.xray_hip: Displaced right femoral neck fracture, Garden type IV

key_learning_points: Shortened + externally rotated leg = hip fracture
Hemiarthroplasty for displaced femoral neck in elderly

patient_responses.fall: I slipped on a rug at home, fell on my right side
patient_responses.pain: Severe pain in my groin and hip
patient_responses.walking: I can't move my leg at all
patient_responses.medical: I have osteoporosis and high blood pressure

feedback.correct_message: Well diagnosed! Femoral neck fracture needs prompt surgery.
feedback.incorrect_message: This is a femoral neck fracture. The shortened externally rotated leg + X-ray confirm it.
feedback.attending_note: Femoral neck fracture: Hemiarthroplasty for displaced fracture in elderly. Operate within 48h. DVT prophylaxis (LMWH). Early mobilization with PT. Treat osteoporosis (bisphosphonates, vitamin D, calcium). Orthogeriatric co-management.
